assumptions: out [7:0]: 8-bit output representing the current state of the ring counter. Only one bit is set high at any time, and the set bit cycles through the 8-bit output.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
Exactly one bit of out is high at any observed cycle.
out cycles through all eight positions in order after reset release.
cases.goal: Observe the full cyclic progression of the ring counter after a clean reset.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Enrichment adds explicit integrity and ordering checks while preserving the original conservative intent.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Assert reset to initialize the counter, release it, then record out on successive clock edges to verify the expected sequence.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Conservative observation on each rising edge of clk; no fixed latency assumed.

expected_properties: When out equals 8'b1000_0000, the next rising edge of clk produces out = 8'b0000_0001
cases.goal: Verify that the ring counter correctly wraps from the most‑significant bit back to the least‑significant bit.
stimulus_intent: Drive a sequence of clock cycles to reach the MSB state, then check the wrap‑around on the next edge.
timing_assumptions: Observe on rising edges of clk; no fixed‑cycle latency assumed.

expected_properties: Asserting reset forces out to 8'b0000_0001 regardless of prior state.
cases.goal: Confirm that an active reset overrides the current count and initializes the counter to the LSB.
stimulus_intent: Run the counter for a few cycles, then assert reset and verify out returns to the initial state.
timing_assumptions: Reset is assumed to be synchronous or asynchronous as per contract; observation is conservative.
